claim: Independent verification confirms: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema correctly separates external research evidence from project code evidence through explicit scope handling and validation rules that prevent external-only evidence from satisfying existing project facts.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() independently verified: implements multi-layer scope classification by checking explicit scope field, URL patterns (http/https), source type prefixes (external_, project_), and path presence. Returns 'project' only for concrete project locators.
evidence.why: This is the core scope classification logic that implements the separation contract.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator() independently verified: validates that project-scoped evidence must have a non-URL path. Returns false for paths starting with http:// or https://, ensuring URLs cannot serve as project evidence locators.
evidence.why: This is the primary defense against URLs being used as project locators.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() independently verified: when evidence lacks project scope but evidence exists, generates PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE; when marked project but lacks path locator, generates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED. These are the enforcement blockers.
evidence.why: These are the enforcement mechanisms that convert validation failures into PRD blockers.

evidence.summary: Scope classification rule independently verified: explicit check for source types containing 'external_web', 'web', 'webfetch', 'websearch', 'internet', 'external_docs', 'external_doc', 'external', 'research' returns 'external' scope.
evidence.why: This confirms that WebSearch and WebFetch sources are classified as external evidence.

evidence.summary: URL detection rule independently verified: explicit regex check /^https?:\/\/i.test(url) or /^https?:\/\/i.test(path) returns 'external' scope, preventing any URL-based evidence from being project-scoped.
evidence.why: This is the URL-based classification rule that separates web research from project files.

evidence.summary: Project source types independently verified: explicit check for source types 'project_code', 'code', 'implementation', 'repo_code', 'project_test', 'test', 'tests', 'fixture', 'fixtures', 'project_docs', 'docs', 'doc', 'documentation', 'project_config', 'config', 'configuration', 'project_log', 'log', 'logs', 'project_artifact', 'artifact' returns 'project' scope.
evidence.why: This confirms the allowed project evidence source types match the documented contract.

evidence.summary: Test case 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' independently verified: evidence with scope=external, source=web-search, url field triggers PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker in readiness check.
evidence.why: This is the behavioral test that proves external evidence rejection for project facts.

evidence.summary: Test case 'project evidence requires a concrete project locator' independently verified: evidence with scope=project but missing path field triggers PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker in readiness check.
evidence.why: This is the behavioral test that proves project evidence without locators is rejected.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt WebFetch/WebSearch instruction independently verified: when toolProfile includes 'research', prompt includes 'WebFetch/WebSearch are allowed for external research; mark those records as scope=external and do not use them as project facts.'
evidence.why: This confirms agents are explicitly instructed to mark WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence.

evidence.summary: Readiness enforcement independently verified: for each evidence agent result, projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() is called and any blockers are added to the PRD readiness blocker list, preventing PRD readiness when evidence scope requirements are not met.
evidence.why: This is the integration point that converts evidence validation failures into PRD readiness blockers.

risks: If this claim is wrong, external research could be mistakenly used as project facts, leading to incorrect PRD readiness decisions and PRDs based on non-existent project states
If scope validation fails, agents could bypass the evidence requirements and generate PRDs for incorrect project states, causing downstream implementation work to target the wrong codebase state

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms the explorer claim is accurate. All 11 evidence points have been verified through independent reads of source files. The scope separation implementation is complete through: (1) multi-layer scope classification logic in evidenceRecordScope() checking explicit scope, URL patterns, source types, and path presence; (2) project locator validation in evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator() rejecting URLs; (3) blocker generation in projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() creating PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE and PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED; (4) agent prompt instructions marking WebFetch/WebSearch as external; (5) readiness enforcement calling projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() for each agent result; (6) behavioral tests proving external-only evidence cannot satisfy project facts and project evidence requires concrete locators. No conflicts or discrepancies found between explorer evidence and independent verification.
